title: Coconut bake
subtitle: Trinidadian coconut flatbread
excerpt: A round, thick flatbread made with desiccated coconut worked into a simple flour dough, cooked in a dry pan or low oven until the outside is golden and slightly firm and the inside is soft and faintly sweet. Coconut bake is the breakfast bread of Trinidad and Tobago, served alongside buljol (salted cod and vegetables), fried bakes, or eggs. The coconut flavour is subtle, present in every bite without being overpowering.
sourceNotes: Coconut bake is a Trinidadian flatbread that appears across Trinidad and Tobago as a breakfast staple. The use of coconut in the dough connects to the broader Caribbean tradition of coconut-enriched breads and flatbreads. It is made at home throughout the country and sold at breakfast spots. The recipe varies between families, some use coconut milk, others desiccated coconut; some bake in an oven, others cook on a tawa.

content.text: Coconut bake can be cooked two ways: in a dry heavy pan on the stovetop, or in an oven at 180°C. The stovetop method produces a more rustic, blistered crust; the oven method produces a more even, lightly golden surface. Both are correct. The stovetop method is faster but requires more attention; the oven method allows you to do other things while it bakes.

The dough should be soft but not sticky. The coconut adds a slight richness that makes it more pliable than a plain flour-and-water dough. Rest it for 15 minutes before shaping, the gluten relaxes and the bake holds its shape better when cooked.

Combine the {{plain-flour}}, {{coconut-desiccated}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Add the {{vegetable-oil}} and rub into the flour mixture. Add warm water gradually (about 140 ml) until the dough comes together in a soft, smooth ball. Knead briefly for 2 minutes. Rest covered for 15 minutes. Shape into a round about 20 cm across and 2 cm thick. To cook on the stovetop: place in a dry heavy pan over low-medium heat, cover with a lid, and cook for 12 to 15 minutes per side until golden. To cook in the oven: place on a baking sheet, brush with water, and bake at 180°C for 25 to 30 minutes until golden. Cool slightly before cutting into wedges.

items.fix: Press to no more than 2 cm. A thicker bake requires more time, check with a skewer; it should come out clean
items.symptom: Raw centre after 30 minutes in the oven

items.fix: Toast the coconut in a dry pan for a minute before adding it to the flour. This brings out the flavour.
Coconut bake on the stovetop needs the lowest heat possible with a lid. It is a very slow cook, 12 to 15 minutes per side over a diffuser or the smallest ring
items.symptom: Surface burning before centre is cooked (stovetop)

content.text: Coconut bake is the bread of a Trinidadian Sunday morning breakfast. Alongside buljol (salt cod dressed with tomatoes, onions, and peppers), fried eggs, avocado, and smoked herring, it forms the breakfast spread that is as traditional in Trinidad as bacon and eggs in Britain. The bake is cut into wedges and used to scoop up the accompaniments. In Britain the Trinidadian diaspora makes coconut bake for weekend breakfasts, a simple bread that takes under an hour from mixing to eating.
